title: Camissonia multijuga
taxonomy: Camissonia multijuga is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Onagraceae. It is a member of the Camissonia genus, which is a group of plants commonly known as suncups. This plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The species is further classified as Camissonia multijuga (Torr. & A. Gray ex Benth.) Jeps., with Jeps. being the author who reclassified it. Camissonia multijuga is a perennial plant that thrives in dry to moist areas.

constituents: Camissonia multijuga, also known as the many-jointed evening primrose, contains various active constituents that contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. intro: Camissonia multijuga, also known as desert starflower, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health due to its antioxidant and vasodilatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Camissonia multijuga include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
